abstract: Accurately performing document-level sentiment classification requires capturing the complex intrinsic relationships between sentences to derive a cohesive semantic representation. To address this challenge, we propose a neural network architecture designed to learn vector-based document representations through a unified, bottom-up approach. Our model first derives sentence-level features using either convolutional neural networks or long short-term memory networks. These representations, along with their inter-sentential dependencies, are then adaptively integrated into a comprehensive document vector using a gated recurrent neural network. We evaluated our approach on four extensive review datasets from IMDB and the Yelp Dataset Challenge. The experimental results demonstrate that our model consistently outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms. Furthermore, we show that gated recurrent architectures significantly exceed the performance of standard recurrent neural networks when modeling document structure for sentiment analysis.
